test.name: Write a not-proved proof argument for two rival candidate fathers
test.description: The flynn-parentage-not-proved scenario has two equally-plausible candidate fathers (Thomas in dwelling 84, Michael in dwelling 210 — both 1850), with the 1860 census (Patrick a boarder, no Flynn present), the death certificate (father blank), and a negative baptism search all failing to break the tie. The skill should write a `not_proved` conclusion as a full Proof Argument laying out both candidates and why neither can be confirmed, and must NOT write tree.gedcomx.json.

input.user_message: Write up the conclusion for Patrick's father — who was it?

judge_context: Should select the `not_proved` tier: after a reasonable search the evidence supports Thomas and Michael about equally and decisively links Patrick to neither — there is no basis to lean either way

Should present the conclusion as a Proof Argument (not a Statement or Summary): competing candidates with only indirect evidence and a failed elimination require the reader to follow the full chain of reasoning
Should present BOTH candidate households and explain why each cannot be confirmed — the 1860 boarder record and the blank death-certificate father field do not bridge to either
Should incorporate the negative evidence (the baptism search returned nothing; the death certificate did not name a father)

Must NOT set a `primary`/concluded value in tree.gedcomx.json — at `not_proved` the skill reaches no conclusion, so during its run it marks no fact `primary` and writes no concluded ParentChild edge (proof-conclusion sets `primary` only at probable+)
